prompt:
A small pan-tilt ultrasonic scanning rig on a workbench: two micro servos mounted in a stacked bracket, wiring neatly routed to a compact microcontroller board, and an ultrasonic rangefinder module mounted front-and-center. The assembly is posed mid-sweep, with a few calibration tools nearby (small screwdriver, jumper wires) to make it unambiguous that this is a sensor rig for scanning distance in two axes.

Ultra-detailed, photorealistic 512×512 image with a grounded near-future maker-lab aesthetic: soft cinematic lighting, realistic metal screws and matte plastics, no brand logos, no readable labels, and no text overlays. Keep the rig centered and clearly readable as a compact robotics sensor assembly.
